formulas.name: Centripetal acceleration
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('v', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: uniform circular motion

formulas.name: Centripetal force
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('m', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('v', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: uniform circular motion

formulas.name: Damped angular frequency
formulas.expr_srepr: Pow(Add(Mul(Integer(-1), Rational(1, 4), Pow(Symbol('b', real=True), Integer(2)), Pow(Symbol('m', real=True), Integer(-2))), Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Pow(Symbol('m', real=True), Integer(-1)))), Rational(1, 2))
assumptions: underdamped (b < 2√(km))
linear damping

formulas.name: Damping ratio
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('b', real=True), Pow(Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True)), Rational(-1, 2)))
assumptions: linear damping

formulas.name: Terminal velocity (linear drag)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Pow(Symbol('b', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True))
assumptions: drag linear in speed
constant gravity

formulas.name: Velocity under linear drag
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Add(Symbol('v0', real=True), Mul(Integer(-1), Pow(Symbol('b', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True))), exp(Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('b', real=True), Pow(Symbol('m', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('t', real=True)))), Mul(Pow(Symbol('b', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True)))
formulas.latex: v = \frac{m g}{b} + \left(- \frac{m g}{b} + v_{0}\right) e^{- \frac{b t}{m}}
assumptions: drag linear in speed
constant gravity

formulas.name: Kinetic friction
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('F_N', real=True), Symbol('mu_k', real=True))
assumptions: surfaces sliding
Coulomb friction model

formulas.name: Hooke's law (spring force)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('k', real=True), Symbol('x', real=True))
assumptions: ideal spring
within the elastic limit

formulas.name: Newton's second law
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('a', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True))
assumptions: F is the net force
m constant

formulas.name: Weight (gravitational force near Earth)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True))

g.desc: gravitational acceleration (signed; -10 in the house convention)

assumptions: uniform gravitational field
g is the signed acceleration (up positive)

formulas.name: Peak EMF of an AC generator
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('A', real=True), Symbol('B', real=True), Symbol('omega', real=True))
assumptions: single-turn coil rotating at constant ω in a uniform field B
peak of ℰ(t) = BAω sin(ωt)

formulas.name: Magnetic field of a long straight wire
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Pow(pi, Integer(-1)), Symbol('I', real=True), Symbol('mu0', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: infinitely long, thin straight wire
field circles the wire; magnitude falls as 1/r

formulas.name: Capacitance
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('Q', real=True), Pow(Symbol('V', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: linear capacitor: charge proportional to voltage
C set by geometry, not by Q or V

formulas.name: Energy stored in a capacitor
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Pow(Symbol('C', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('Q', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: ideal capacitor
equals ½CV² = ½QV via Q = CV
the area under the voltage–charge line (a triangle)

formulas.name: Coulomb's law
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Symbol('q1', real=True), Symbol('q2', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-2)))
assumptions: point charges (or spherical charge distributions)
magnitude of the force; like charges repel, unlike attract

formulas.name: Electric field of a point charge
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Symbol('q', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-2)))
assumptions: point charge (or spherical charge)
magnitude of the radial field; points away from a positive charge

formulas.name: Faraday's law of induction
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('dPhidt', real=True))

dPhidt.desc: rate of change of magnetic flux dΦ/dt

assumptions: single-turn loop (multiply by N turns)
the minus sign is Lenz's law: the EMF opposes the change in flux

formulas.name: Magnetic flux
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('A', real=True), Symbol('B', real=True))
assumptions: uniform field perpendicular to a flat area A
general case Φ = ∫B·dA

formulas.name: Inductor EMF
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('L', real=True), Symbol('dIdt', real=True))

dIdt.desc: rate of change of current dI/dt

assumptions: ideal inductor
the self-induced EMF opposes the change in current (Lenz's law)

formulas.name: LC angular frequency
formulas.expr_srepr: Pow(Mul(Symbol('C', real=True), Symbol('L', real=True)), Rational(-1, 2))
assumptions: ideal LC circuit, no resistance
free oscillation L Q'' + Q/C = 0

formulas.name: LC period
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(2), pi, Pow(Mul(Symbol('C', real=True), Symbol('L', real=True)), Rational(1, 2)))
assumptions: ideal LC circuit, no resistance
free oscillation at ω = 1/√(LC)

formulas.name: Magnetic force on a charge
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('B', real=True), Symbol('q', real=True), Symbol('v', real=True))
assumptions: magnitude for velocity perpendicular to the field
force is perpendicular to both v and B (F = qv×B)

formulas.name: Motional EMF
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('B', real=True), Symbol('L', real=True), Symbol('v', real=True))
assumptions: straight conductor of length L moving at speed v across a field B
a special case of Faraday's law, EMF = −dΦ/dt, with Φ = BLx

formulas.name: Ohm's law
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('I', real=True), Symbol('R', real=True))
assumptions: ohmic conductor: resistance R independent of current
steady current

formulas.name: Electric potential energy of two charges
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Symbol('q1', real=True), Symbol('q2', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: point charges
energy taken zero at infinite separation
the work to assemble the charges, ∫F dr

formulas.name: Electric potential of a point charge
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Symbol('q', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: point charge
potential taken zero at infinity
the area under the field to infinity converges (1/r²)

formulas.name: Electrical power
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('I', real=True), Symbol('V', real=True))
assumptions: power delivered to a circuit element
equals I²R = V²/R for a resistor (Ohm's law)

formulas.name: Charge on a charging capacitor (RC circuit)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('C', real=True), Symbol('V', real=True), Add(Integer(1), Mul(Integer(-1), exp(Mul(Integer(-1), Pow(Symbol('C', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('R', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('t', real=True))))))
assumptions: series RC circuit charged from a constant EMF
capacitor initially uncharged

formulas.name: RC time constant
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('C', real=True), Symbol('R', real=True))
assumptions: series resistor and capacitor
the charge approaches its final value as 1 − e^{−t/τ}

formulas.name: Magnetic force on a current-carrying wire
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('B', real=True), Symbol('I', real=True), Symbol('L', real=True))

L.desc: length of wire in the field

assumptions: straight wire perpendicular to the field
uniform field over the length L

formulas.name: Kinetic energy
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('m', real=True), Pow(Symbol('v', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: speeds well below light speed

formulas.name: Mechanical energy (kinetic + gravitational potential)
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('h', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True)), Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('m', real=True), Pow(Symbol('v', real=True), Integer(2))))
assumptions: conserved when only conservative forces (gravity) do work
no friction or other dissipation

formulas.name: Mechanical power (constant force)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('F', real=True), Symbol('v', real=True))
assumptions: force parallel to velocity

formulas.name: Elastic potential energy
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('k', real=True), Pow(Symbol('x', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: ideal spring (Hooke's law)

formulas.name: Work (constant force along displacement)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('F', real=True), Symbol('d', real=True))
assumptions: constant force parallel to displacement

formulas.name: Bernoulli's equation (total pressure)
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Symbol('P', real=True), Mul(Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('h', real=True), Symbol('rho', real=True)), Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('rho', real=True), Pow(Symbol('v', real=True), Integer(2))))
assumptions: steady, incompressible, non-viscous flow along a streamline
energy conservation per unit volume; P₀ is constant along the streamline

formulas.name: Buoyant force (Archimedes' principle)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('Vdisp', real=True), Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('rho', real=True))
assumptions: body fully or partly submerged in a fluid of density ρ
buoyant force equals the weight of displaced fluid

formulas.name: Continuity (volume flow rate)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('A', real=True), Symbol('v', real=True))
assumptions: incompressible fluid
Q = Av is conserved along a streamtube, so a narrower pipe means faster flow

formulas.name: Hydrostatic force on a vertical wall
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Pow(Symbol('H', real=True), Integer(2)), Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('rho', real=True), Symbol('w', real=True))
assumptions: incompressible fluid of constant density
flat vertical wall of constant width, top edge at the surface
gauge pressure (atmospheric cancels across the wall)
the area under the pressure–depth profile, ∫P w dh

formulas.name: Hydrostatic pressure with depth
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('g', real=True), Symbol('h', real=True), Symbol('rho', real=True))
assumptions: incompressible fluid of constant density
gauge pressure (above the surface); add atmospheric for absolute

formulas.name: Newton's law of universal gravitation
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('G', real=True), Symbol('m1', real=True), Symbol('m2', real=True), Pow(Symbol('r', real=True), Integer(-2)))
assumptions: point masses (or spherical bodies)
magnitude of the attractive force

formulas.name: Constant acceleration
formulas.expr_srepr: Symbol('a', real=True)
formulas.note: The seed of the integral ladder: hold $a$ constant and the velocity $v=\int a\,dt$ and position $x=\int v\,dt$ accumulate from it — the constant-acceleration formulas are those integrals, evaluated.
assumptions: acceleration is constant

formulas.name: Velocity under constant acceleration
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Symbol('a', real=True), Symbol('t', real=True)), Symbol('v0', real=True))
assumptions: acceleration is constant

formulas.name: Position under constant acceleration
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('a', real=True), Pow(Symbol('t', real=True), Integer(2))), Mul(Symbol('t', real=True), Symbol('v0', real=True)), Symbol('x0', real=True))
assumptions: acceleration is constant

formulas.name: de Broglie wavelength
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('h', real=True), Pow(Symbol('p', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: matter has a wavelength set by its momentum
h is Planck's constant

formulas.name: Radioactive decay law
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('N0', real=True), exp(Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('lam', real=True), Symbol('t', real=True))))
assumptions: a large number of identical nuclei, each decaying independently
λ is the decay constant (probability of decay per unit time)

formulas.name: Half-life
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Pow(Symbol('lam', real=True), Integer(-1)), log(Integer(2)))
assumptions: exponential decay with decay constant λ

formulas.name: Mass–energy equivalence
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Pow(Symbol('c', real=True), Integer(2)), Symbol('m', real=True))
assumptions: rest energy of a mass m
c is the speed of light

formulas.name: Photoelectric effect
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Symbol('f', real=True), Symbol('h', real=True)), Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('phi', real=True)))
assumptions: one photon ejects one electron
no current below the threshold frequency f = φ/h, regardless of intensity

formulas.name: Photon energy
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('f', real=True), Symbol('h', real=True))
assumptions: light comes in quanta of energy hf
h is Planck's constant

formulas.name: Impulse (constant force)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('F', real=True), Symbol('t', real=True))
assumptions: constant force over the interval

formulas.name: Perfectly inelastic collision (common velocity)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Pow(Add(Symbol('m1', real=True), Symbol('m2', real=True)), Integer(-1)), Add(Mul(Symbol('m1', real=True), Symbol('v1', real=True)), Mul(Symbol('m2', real=True), Symbol('v2', real=True))))
assumptions: isolated 1D collision (momentum conserved)
bodies move off together (e = 0)

formulas.name: Linear momentum
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('m', real=True), Symbol('v', real=True))
assumptions: speeds well below light speed

formulas.name: Magnification (thin lens or mirror)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('di', real=True), Pow(Symbol('do', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: thin lens or mirror, paraxial rays
negative m means an inverted image; |m| > 1 is enlarged

formulas.name: Snell's law of refraction
formulas.expr_srepr: asin(Mul(Symbol('n1', real=True), Pow(Symbol('n2', real=True), Integer(-1)), sin(Symbol('theta1', real=True))))

n1.desc: refractive index of the first medium

n2.desc: refractive index of the second medium

assumptions: light crossing a flat boundary between two media
n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂; angles measured from the normal

formulas.name: Thin-lens equation
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Pow(Symbol('do', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('di', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: thin lens, paraxial rays
sign convention: real images at positive dᵢ

formulas.name: Kepler's third law (circular orbit)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(2), pi, Pow(Mul(Pow(Symbol('G', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('M', real=True), Integer(-1)), Pow(Symbol('R', real=True), Integer(3))), Rational(1, 2)))
assumptions: circular orbit
the orbiting mass cancels
T² = 4π²R³/GM — the period squared scales as the radius cubed

formulas.name: Circular orbital speed
formulas.expr_srepr: Pow(Mul(Symbol('G', real=True), Symbol('M', real=True), Pow(Symbol('R', real=True), Integer(-1))), Rational(1, 2))
assumptions: circular orbit
central body of mass M; the orbiting mass cancels
gravity supplies the centripetal force, GM/R² = v²/R

formulas.name: Angular momentum (rigid body)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('I', real=True), Symbol('omega', real=True))
assumptions: rotation about a fixed axis

formulas.name: Rotational kinetic energy
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('I', real=True), Pow(Symbol('omega', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: rotation about a fixed axis

formulas.name: Moment of inertia of a disk
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Symbol('M', real=True), Pow(Symbol('R', real=True), Integer(2)))
assumptions: uniform solid disk (or cylinder), axis through the centre
from I = ∫r² dm with mass spread over area

formulas.name: Moment of inertia of a rod (about one end)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 3), Pow(Symbol('L', real=True), Integer(2)), Symbol('M', real=True))
assumptions: uniform thin rod, axis through one end
derived from I = ∫r² dm; ⅓ML² about the centre is ML²/12

formulas.name: Parallel-axis theorem
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Symbol('Icm', real=True), Mul(Symbol('M', real=True), Pow(Symbol('d', real=True), Integer(2))))

Icm.desc: moment of inertia about the centre of mass

d.desc: distance between the two parallel axes

assumptions: axis parallel to one through the centre of mass, offset by d
Icm is the moment about the centre-of-mass axis

formulas.name: Torque (force at a lever arm)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('F', real=True), Symbol('r', real=True))
assumptions: force perpendicular to the lever arm

formulas.name: Rotational work (constant torque through an angle)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('tau', real=True), Symbol('theta', real=True))
assumptions: constant torque about a fixed axis
the area under the torque–angle curve, ∫τ dθ

formulas.name: Angular frequency of a spring–mass oscillator
formulas.expr_srepr: Pow(Mul(Symbol('k', real=True), Pow(Symbol('m', real=True), Integer(-1))), Rational(1, 2))
assumptions: ideal Hooke's-law spring
no damping

formulas.name: Period of a spring–mass oscillator
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(2), pi, Pow(Symbol('omega', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: ideal Hooke's-law spring
no damping

formulas.name: Position in simple harmonic motion
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Symbol('x0', real=True), cos(Mul(Symbol('omega', real=True), Symbol('t', real=True)))), Mul(Pow(Symbol('omega', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('v0', real=True), sin(Mul(Symbol('omega', real=True), Symbol('t', real=True)))))
assumptions: ideal Hooke's-law spring
no damping

formulas.name: Adiabatic relation (PVγ = const)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('P1', real=True), Pow(Mul(Symbol('V1', real=True), Pow(Symbol('V2', real=True), Integer(-1))), Symbol('gamma', real=True)))
assumptions: reversible adiabatic process: PVγ = const
ideal gas with constant heat-capacity ratio γ = Cp/Cv

formulas.name: Adiabatic work
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('Cv', real=True), Symbol('n', real=True), Add(Symbol('T1', real=True), Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('T2', real=True))))

Cv.desc: molar heat capacity at constant volume

assumptions: adiabatic process: no heat exchanged (Q = 0), so W = −ΔU = nCv(T₁−T₂)
equivalently W = (P₁V₁ − P₂V₂)/(γ−1); the gas cools as it does work

formulas.name: Carnot efficiency
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('Tc', real=True), Pow(Symbol('Th', real=True), Integer(-1))), Integer(1))
assumptions: reversible (Carnot) engine between two reservoirs
the maximum efficiency any heat engine can reach between Tc and Th

formulas.name: First law of thermodynamics
formulas.expr_srepr: Add(Symbol('Q', real=True), Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('W', real=True)))
assumptions: energy conservation for a closed system
sign convention: Q added to the gas positive, W done by the gas positive

formulas.name: Heat and specific heat
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('c', real=True), Symbol('dT', real=True), Symbol('m', real=True))
assumptions: specific heat c constant over the temperature range
no phase change

formulas.name: Ideal-gas law (pressure form)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('R', real=True), Symbol('T', real=True), Pow(Symbol('V', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('n', real=True))
assumptions: ideal gas (no intermolecular forces, point particles)

formulas.name: Internal energy of a monatomic ideal gas
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(3, 2), Symbol('R', real=True), Symbol('T', real=True), Symbol('n', real=True))
assumptions: ideal gas
monatomic (3 translational degrees of freedom); use 5/2 for diatomic

formulas.name: Isobaric work (constant pressure)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('P', real=True), Add(Mul(Integer(-1), Symbol('V1', real=True)), Symbol('V2', real=True)))
assumptions: constant pressure (isobaric process)
work is the area under the P–V curve, W = ∫P dV — here a rectangle

formulas.name: Isothermal work (ideal gas)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('R', real=True), Symbol('T', real=True), Symbol('n', real=True), log(Mul(Pow(Symbol('V1', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('V2', real=True))))
assumptions: ideal gas at constant temperature (isothermal, reversible expansion)
work is the area under the P–V curve, W = ∫P dV

formulas.name: Period and frequency
formulas.expr_srepr: Pow(Symbol('f', real=True), Integer(-1))
assumptions: periodic motion or wave
period is the time for one cycle; frequency is cycles per second

formulas.name: Wave speed
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Symbol('f', real=True), Symbol('lam', real=True))
assumptions: periodic wave
v set by the medium; frequency and wavelength trade off at fixed v

formulas.name: Standing-wave harmonics (string fixed at both ends)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Rational(1, 2), Pow(Symbol('L', real=True), Integer(-1)), Symbol('n', real=True), Symbol('v', real=True))
assumptions: string fixed at both ends (a node at each end)
n = 1, 2, 3, … selects the harmonic

formulas.name: Standing-wave wavelengths (string fixed at both ends)
formulas.expr_srepr: Mul(Integer(2), Symbol('L', real=True), Pow(Symbol('n', real=True), Integer(-1)))
assumptions: string fixed at both ends (a node at each end)
an integer number of half-wavelengths fits the length

formulas.name: Speed of a wave on a string
formulas.expr_srepr: Pow(Mul(Symbol('FT', real=True), Pow(Symbol('mu', real=True), Integer(-1))), Rational(1, 2))
assumptions: transverse wave on a uniform string
small-amplitude (linear) waves
